title: Seeing all stages and what's undecided
question: How do we show the whole SDLC at a glance, including stages with no decisions yet?
rationale: A slim rail under the hero shows all six lifecycle stages in order with their counts and doubles as a jump-to-stage nav. Empty stages render muted as "none yet", so coverage gaps show without a separate panel — the review-first way to surface what hasn't been decided.

options.label: One rail: stages + counts + jump nav, empty = "none yet"
options.note: A single band combines the SDLC spine, per-stage counts, click-to-jump, and the coverage cue (empty stages muted).

tradeoffs.note: surfaces gaps and the lifecycle arc without hunting
nav and coverage in a single band, not two competing surfaces
modest — one component, jump + scroll wiring

options.label: A separate coverage callout, no nav

tradeoffs.note: shows gaps but adds nothing for navigation
another box to place and maintain — more clutter

options.label: Only show stages that have decisions

tradeoffs.note: hides the empty stages — which is exactly the coverage signal we want
nothing extra, but it defeats the purpose
